dry_bulk.confluence_note: Extreme quantitative strength (BDI Z-score +2.24) and positive momentum clash with persistently negative fundamentals in the Chinese steel sector.
dry_bulk.summary: Spot rates across all dry classes have reached the 99th percentile of 5-year levels, led by a Capesize surge of 65.9% over the last 60 days. This expansion occurs despite bearish underlying data, including a 3.6% YOY decline in Chinese steel production and record-high iron ore inventories at 166mt.
dry_bulk.outlook: Bullish technical momentum is expected to maintain elevated spot levels in the immediate term despite the fragile fundamental floor.
dry_bulk.watch: Chinese steel production revisions and potential iron ore inventory destocking trends.

tanker.confluence_note: Record-breaking clean tanker rates (Z=+3.21) are fundamentally supported by geopolitical disruptions and unnavigable straits.
tanker.summary: The tanker market is in a super-expansionary regime, with clean rates up 147% over 60 days as trade routes reorganize around geopolitical blockages. While recent momentum has turned negative—suggesting the initial 'panic bid' is cooling—the positive fundamental outlook remains intact due to drawing inventories. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2198.0 (ROC60 +147.0%), alongside dirty tankers at 2854.0 (ROC60 +75.0%).
key_signals: Global Crude Oil On Water -0.4% YOY
Middle East-Asia VLCC rates up 71.1% YOY
tanker.outlook: Rates are projected to remain at historically high levels as long as maritime chokepoints remain restricted.
tanker.watch: Re-opening of key straits and US crude oil export volume recovery.

macro_note: Sustained high oil prices and geopolitical volatility are currently the primary drivers of shipping freight, acting as a tailwind for tanker demand while increasing bunker costs and inflationary risks for dry bulk.
